Research indicates that the top-performing stocks often show earnings acceleration before their share prices increase. To that end, Intuit Inc. INTU, The Goldman Sachs Group, Inc. GS, and Octave Specialty Group, Inc. OSG are showing  ...    Earnings acceleration is the incremental growth in a company’s earnings per share (EPS). In other words, if a company’s quarter-over-quarter earnings growth rate increases within a stipulated time frame, it can be called earnings acceleration. In the case of earnings growth, you pay for something that is already reflected in the stock price. However, earnings acceleration helps identify stocks that haven’t yet caught investors' attention and, once secured, will invariably lead  ...    rates. EPS % Growth (Q-1)/(Q-2) greater than EPS % Growth (Q-2)/(Q-3): The growth rate from one quarter ago (Q-1) over two quarters ago (Q-2) has to be greater than the growth rate from two quarters ago (Q-2) over three quarters ago (Q-3). results.title: Nvidia Posts Blockbuster Fiscal 2026 Results – Valuentum
results.author: Brian Nelson
highlights: with revenue and non-GAAP earnings per share exceeding the respective consensus forecasts. The company posted record revenue for the fourth quarter of $68.1 billion, up 20% sequentially and up 73% from the year-ago period. Data Center revenue in the quarter was $62.3 billion, up 22% sequentially and up 75% from the same period a year ago. For the quarter, GAAP and non-GAAP gross margins were 75.0% and 75.2%, respectively, while GAAP and non-GAAP earnings per share were $1.76 and $1.62, respectively.  ...    Computing demand is growing exponentially — the agentic AI inflection point has arrived. Grace Blackwell with NVLink is the king of inference today — delivering an order-of-magnitude lower cost per token — and Vera Rubin will extend that leadership even further. Enterprise adoption of agents is skyrocketing. Our customers are racing to invest in AI  ...    During fiscal 2026, Nvidia returned $41.1 billion to shareholders in the form of buybacks and cash dividend payments. Looking to the first quarter of fiscal 2027, Nvidia expects revenue to be $78 billion, plus or minus 2%, well above the consensus estimate of $72 billion at the time and a forecast that does not assume any Data Center compute revenue from China. GAAP and non-GAAP gross margins are expected to be 74.9% and 75.0%, respectively, plus or minus 50 basis points. In the quarter, GAAP and non-GAAP operating expenses are expected to be approximately $7.7 billion and $7.5 billion, respectively. Nvidia ended the quarter with $62.6 billion in cash and marketable securities versus $8.5 billion in short- and long-term debt. We continue to like Nvidia in the Best Ideas Newsletter portfolio. —– Brian Nelson owns shares in SPY, SCHG, QQQ, QQQM, DIA, VOT, RSP, and IWM. Valuentum owns SPY, SCHG, QQQ, QQQM, VOO, and DIA. FAQ: Addressing Practical Concerns

### Should I buy these top performers now, after their March gains? No — not as a reaction to past performance. These stocks are trading at premiums reflecting their demonstrated execution capability. Instead, monitor their upcoming catalysts: NIO’s Q2 delivery guidance (due May 8), Enphase’s EU distribution agreement announcements (expected April 15), and Vertex’s VX-548 launch metrics (patient enrollment data due June 3). Enter on confirmation of continued operational delivery, not momentum. ### Why weren’t any traditional tech giants (AAPL, MSFT) in the top five? They delivered solid returns — Apple +6.1%, Microsoft +5.8% — but lagged because their March catalysts were incremental (iOS 19 beta release, Azure AI inference optimizations), not structural.
